source_derivation.visual_evidence: A grounded solo dance built from torso isolations, leg accents, sweeping arms and a wide-stance peak calls for a beat with pocket rather than maximal density.
source_derivation.audible_evidence: Both camera takes contain continuous rhythmic playback. A source-only onset-envelope pass was noisy because of room capture, but source-b produced a leading estimate near 108 BPM; movement accents also read naturally in a 106-110 BPM pocket.

music_archetype.name: bass-forward pop/R&B groove with Afro-pop or funk percussion
avoid: lyrics that fight the three short text cards

energy_arc.music_need: recognizable downbeat plus two compact impact pairs
steady groove with room around the snare
new layer, bass answer, or vocal pickup
clear accent under the knee rise and a clean last beat

output_time_anchors.picture: impact crop opens into the groove

candidate_segment.offset_basis: Apple catalog preview-relative; not a verified full-track or Instagram-library offset
candidate_segment.alignment: Place the first strong preview downbeat at output 00:00.060. This balances sub-frame drift across the 17.8-second edit.

selected_candidates.why_it_fits: The analyzed preview has a very stable approximately 108 BPM onset grid and the uncluttered, syncopated groove leaves room for the dancer's isolations.
selected_candidates.instagram_availability: UNVERIFIED — user must confirm this exact recording in their regional Instagram music library and manually locate the matching phrase.
selected_candidates.rights_note: Use only through Instagram's licensed music tool or another properly licensed workflow. The catalog preview is not embedded in source-only-preview.mp4.

handoff_steps: In Instagram, search exactly for 'Woman' by Doja Cat and verify the artist/recording.
Scrub to a steady groove phrase matching the catalog-preview segment; because Apple does not expose the preview's full-track offset, locate it by ear.
Align the first strong downbeat to output 00:00.060, then check the critical anchors at 00:02.267, 00:06.733, 00:11.200, 00:15.667 and 00:17.767.
Mute or remove the synthetic metronome before publishing.
If the exact recording is unavailable, use the archetype/search phrases and prioritize tempo plus the critical anchors over title similarity.
